algorithms.title: Electrical Therapy Selection in ACLS
algorithms.description: Decision pathway for selecting the appropriate electrical therapy (defibrillation, synchronized cardioversion, or pacing) based on the clinical scenario and rhythm.
steps: Cardiac arrest or arrhythmia identified — determine clinical context.
Is the patient in cardiac arrest (no pulse, no breathing)?
→ YES: Assess rhythm:
→ VF or pulseless VT → SHOCKABLE: Defibrillate
→ Biphasic: 120–200J (manufacturer's recommendation; if unknown, 200J)
→ Monophasic: 360J
→ Resume CPR for 2 minutes; reassess
→ Asystole or PEA → NON-SHOCKABLE: CPR + epinephrine + treat causes; do NOT defibrillate or cardiovert
→ NO: Patient has a pulse — assess stability:
→ Hemodynamically UNSTABLE (hypotension, altered mental status, chest pain, acute heart failure):
→ Narrow-regular SVT → Synchronized cardioversion at 100J biphasic
→ Monomorphic VT with pulse → Synchronized cardioversion at 100J biphasic
→ Atrial fibrillation/flutter → Synchronized cardioversion at ≥200J biphasic
→ Polymorphic VT → UNSYNCHRONIZED defibrillation at 120–200J biphasic (sync unreliable)
→ Hemodynamically STABLE:
→ Attempt medical therapy first (vagal maneuvers, adenosine for SVT; amiodarone for VT)
→ Consider elective synchronized cardioversion with sedation if medications fail
→ Symptomatic bradycardia refractory to atropine → Transcutaneous pacing (start 80 mA, titrate by 10 mA q2–3 min, max 200 mA)
→ Tachyarrhythmia amenable to overdrive pacing → ATP (pace at 10–20% above tachycardia rate)

algorithms.title: Transcutaneous Pacing Protocol
algorithms.description: Step-by-step protocol for initiating and titrating transcutaneous pacing in adults with hemodynamically significant bradycardia.
steps: Hemodynamically significant bradycardia identified (HR <50, symptomatic, hypotensive).
Administer atropine 1 mg IV; repeat q3–5 min up to 3 mg total.
If atropine ineffective → prepare for transcutaneous pacing.
Apply anterior-posterior pacing pads per manufacturer instructions.
Set device to demand (pacer) mode at 80 mA.
Observe monitor for electrical capture (wide QRS complex following each pacer spike).
If no electrical capture after 2–3 minutes → increase current by 10 mA (to 90 mA).
Repeat: increase by 10 mA every 2–3 minutes until electrical capture achieved.
When electrical capture achieved: immediately confirm mechanical capture (palpate pulse with each paced complex).
If mechanical capture confirmed: TCP effective — arrange transvenous pacing or cardiology consultation.
If electrical capture without mechanical capture: continue CPR/resuscitation efforts and escalate to transvenous pacing.
Maximum output: 200 mA — if no capture at 200 mA, TCP has failed → transvenous pacing or cardiology emergency.
Provide sedation/analgesia for conscious patients (TCP is painful).
Continuously monitor rhythm, blood pressure, and patient comfort.
